title: Introduction to dplyr and the Pipe
description: Learn about the dplyr package for data manipulation and the pipe operator %>%.

slides.title: What is dplyr? 🛠️
slides.content:
# dplyr: A Grammar of Data Manipulation

dplyr is one of the most popular R packages for data manipulation. It provides a set of **verbs** that solve the most common data manipulation challenges:

- `filter()` – pick rows based on conditions
- `select()` – pick columns by name
- `mutate()` – add new variables
- `summarise()` – collapse to summary statistics
- `arrange()` – reorder rows
- `group_by()` – operate by group

These verbs work consistently and can be combined to perform complex operations.

slides.title: Installing and Loading dplyr
slides.content:
dplyr is part of the **tidyverse**. You can install the whole tidyverse or just dplyr:

```r
install.packages("dplyr")         # just dplyr
# or
install.packages("tidyverse")      # entire collection
```

Load it with:

```r
library(dplyr)
```

We'll also use the pipe operator `%>%`, which comes with dplyr (or from the magrittr package).

slides.title: Load dplyr and Explore Data
slides.content: We'll use the built-in `mtcars` dataset. First, load dplyr and take a peek:

slides.title: The Pipe Operator %>%
slides.content:
The pipe `%>%` takes the result on its left and passes it as the first argument to the function on its right. This allows you to chain operations in a readable way.

Without pipe:
```r
summarise(group_by(filter(mtcars, mpg > 20), cyl), mean_mpg = mean(mpg))
```

With pipe:
```r
mtcars %>%
  filter(mpg > 20) %>%
  group_by(cyl) %>%
  summarise(mean_mpg = mean(mpg))
```

Read as: take mtcars, then filter, then group, then summarise.

slides.title: First Pipe Example
slides.codeSnippet:
library(dplyr)
mtcars %>%
  filter(mpg > 20) %>%
  select(mpg, cyl, hp) %>%
  head()

quizData.question: What does the pipe operator `%>%` do?

options.text: It creates a sequence of numbers
It passes the left-hand side as the first argument to the right-hand function
It is used for matrix multiplication

quizData.explanation: The pipe forwards the result along the pipeline, making code more readable.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content: Keyboard shortcut for `%>%` in RStudio: `Ctrl+Shift+M` (Cmd+Shift+M on Mac).
